claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for your three children, I'll need to gather some details about each of them and your circumstances.

Let's start with: **What are the names and ages of your three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My three children are Alex, who is 20, Blake, who is 16, and Charlie, who is 5 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about their current situations:

**Is Alex (who is 20 years old) currently in any approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They've actually been in hospital for a few months now, but I still regularly spend money on things they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I understand Alex has been in hospital for a few months. That's an important detail.

Now let me ask about Blake: **Is Blake (who is 16 years old) currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake just left school recently and has signed up with the careers service to figure out what to do next. But they're also working full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Just to clarify about Blake's work situation: **How many hours per week is Blake working in their full-time job?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're working at least 24 hours a week - it's a proper full-time job.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Now I need to ask about your living situation and responsibility for the children:

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, in Leeds. Alex and Charlie live with me here, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Blake doesn't live with you, I need to understand if you're contributing to their upkeep:

**Are you paying towards Blake's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation), and if so, how much do you contribute?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now, just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you a UK resident with the right to reside in the UK, and is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK resident and I have the right to reside here. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just confirm one final detail about Alex's hospital stay:

**How long has Alex been in hospital - is it more or less than 12 weeks? And can you confirm you're regularly spending money on them during their hospital stay?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been in hospital for 13 weeks now. And yes, I can confirm I'm regularly spending money on things they need - like clothes, magazines, and their favorite snacks.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all the information. Based on your circumstances, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility for each of your three children:

**Charlie (5 years old): ELIGIBLE ✓**
You can claim Child Benefit for Charlie. They are under 16, live with you in the UK, and you meet all the standard eligibility requirements.

**Alex (20 years old): NOT ELIGIBLE ✗**
Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex. While they have been in hospital for 13 weeks and you're regularly spending money on them, the hospital exception only applies when the child would otherwise be eligible. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, they don't meet the basic age criteria for Child Benefit (which requires children over 16 to be under 20 AND in approved education or training).

**Blake (16 years old): NOT ELIGIBLE ✗**
Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. Although they've registered with the careers service (which could allow Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks after leaving education), Blake has started paid work for 24 hours or more per week. The rules state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts working 24+ hours per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Additionally, Blake doesn't live with you and you're not contributing to their upkeep, so you wouldn't be considered responsible for them under Child Benefit rules.

**Summary:** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie only. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit for Charlie.

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally only qualify for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. While Alex has been in hospital for 13 weeks and you are regularly spending money on them, the exception for children in hospital applies when they would otherwise be eligible. Since Alex is over 20 or not in approved education/training at age 20, they do not meet the basic age eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 16 years old and has left education. Although Blake has registered with the careers service (which could allow Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks), Blake has started paid work for 24 hours or more per week. Under the rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Additionally, Blake does not live with you and you are not contributing financially towards their upkeep, so you would not be considered responsible for Blake under the Child Benefit rules.

Charlie is 5 years old, which is under the age limit of 16. Charlie lives with you in the UK, meaning you are responsible for them. You are a UK resident with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident living in Leeds with three children: Alex (20), Blake (16), and Charlie (5). Only Charlie qualifies for Child Benefit as they are under 16 and live with the claimant. Alex does not qualify because they are 20 and not in approved education or training, which is required for those over 16. Blake does not qualify because they are working 24+ hours per week (which disqualifies them), do not live with the claimant, and the claimant is not contributing to their upkeep.

Only Charlie qualifies for Child Benefit as they are under 16 and live with the claimant. Alex does not qualify because they are 20 and not in approved education or training, which is required for those over 16. Blake does not qualify because they are working 24+ hours per week (which disqualifies them), do not live with the claimant, and the claimant is not contributing to their upkeep.
